statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: Input consists of multiple test cases. For each test case, the first line of input contains two integers n and m separated by space, where n is the total cost of purchasing and m is the number of coins the customer has. The second line contains m spaces separated integers representing the values of the coins the customer has. It is guaranteed that the sum of m will not exceed 50,000 and the maximum value of any coin is 1,000,000,000.
statement.outputFormat: Print Yes if the customer can buy all the items otherwise print No.
statement.notes: You may assume that the prices of all items are non-negative and that the customer has at least one coin.

constraints.description: Number of coins the customer has

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: bool solve(vector<int>& c, int n, int m); };
starterCode.java: public class Solution { public boolean solve(int[] c, int n, int m); }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, c: list[int], n: int, m: int) -> bool:

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: bool solve(vector<int>& c, int n, int m) { int i = 0; int sum = 0; while (i < m) { sum += c[i]; i++; } return sum >= n; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public boolean solve(int[] c, int n, int m) { int i = 0; int sum = 0; while (i < m) { sum += c[i]; i++; } return sum >= n; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, c: list[int], n: int, m: int) -> bool: i = 0; sum = 0; while i < m: sum += c[i]; i += 1; return sum >= n;
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function solve(nums) { let i = 0; let sum = 0; while (i < nums.length) { sum += nums[i]; i++; } return sum >= target; }

functionSignature.cpp: bool solve(vector<int>& c, int n, int m);
functionSignature.java: boolean solve(int[] c, int n, int m);
functionSignature.python: def solve(self, c: list[int], n: int, m: int) -> bool:

solutions.python:
from collections import defaultdict
from sys import stdin

class Solution:
  def solve(self, c: list[int], n: int, m: int) -> bool:
    # read input
    c = list(map(int, stdin.readline().split()))
    n, m = map(int, stdin.readline().split())

    sum = 0
    for num in c:
      sum += num

    return sum >= n

if __name__ == '__main__':
  for _ in range(int(stdin.readline())):
    c = list(map(int, stdin.readline().split()))
    n, m = map(int, stdin.readline().split())
    s = Solution()
    print('Yes' if s.solve(c, n, m) else 'No')
